message:
Cache wakers across polls to avoid repeated Arc<WorkStealingWaker> allocation

The work-stealing scheduler was creating a fresh Waker (and underlying
Arc<WorkStealingWaker>) on every poll of every task, even though the
waker fields (task_id, global queue, local queue, parker) are immutable
for the lifetime of a task. This added unnecessary allocation pressure
on hot polling paths.

Changes:
- Thread cached_waker through the task record extraction path in
  Worker::execute_task(), taking it from the TaskRecord on each poll
  and storing it back after the future yields
- Reuse the cached waker when available instead of constructing a new
  Arc<WorkStealingWaker> each time; only allocate on first poll when
  no cache exists
- Cache waker back into the TaskRecord for both global and local task
  variants after a Pending yield, so subsequent polls reuse it
- Remove unused all_ids Vec accumulation in obligation_table test
  (replaced with let _ = id to suppress warning without losing coverage)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
